channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The AI church with no Christ
item.description: A note before we start: What follows is fiction. A thought experiment set in an alternate world that only looks like ours by accident. Any resemblance to real labs, real bosses, or real workplace...

item.title: Why ML accuracy numbers are unfalsifiable, and what a 1287-line Python tool does about it" published: false
item.description: A few weeks ago I was reading a model card for an open-weight code model. It claimed pass@1 = 67% on HumanEval. I tried to reproduce it. I got 54%. I went back to the model card. The metric was...

item.title: Financial Custody: Managing Balance and Portfolio with Eventual Consistency
item.description: Hey folks! Continuing the My Broker B3 series, we've reached one of the most business-logic-rich services in the ecosystem: the Broker Wallet API. In previous posts we built the B3 infrastructure...

item.title: How to Extend Filament v5's Register Page
item.description: Filament v5 ships a native register page. Some plugins ignore it and roll their own controller. Here's how we added captcha, honeypot, role assignment, and event bridging — entirely through...

item.title: PageSpeed Is Lying to You - And Your Users Know It
item.description: A fast website doesn’t always mean a good website. I’ve seen this many times in real projects. PageSpeed score — 90+. Everything looks “optimized”. But when you check analytics — it tells a...

item.title: Expand-Contract Pattern vs Blue-Green Deployment for PostgreSQL Schema Migrations
item.description: --- title: "Expand-Contract vs Blue-Green: PostgreSQL Zero-Downtime Migrations" published: true description: "Compare expand-contract pattern and blue-green deployment for PostgreSQL schema...

item.title: Elon Musk had a bad week in court
item.description: Elon Musk is the one who wanted this trial. He has spent months claiming OpenAI "stole a nonprofit," and saying he was the actual driving force behind one of the most important companies currently...

item.title: How Many Log Lines Should You Send to Gemini? The Context Window Problem.
item.description: All tests run on an 8-year-old MacBook Air. When you send logcat lines to Gemini for diagnosis, how many lines should you include? Too few: the model lacks context, gives a generic answer. Too...

item.title: Building a Local AI Workstation: Orchestrating Claude MCP, Cursor, and GitHub for "Vibe Coding"
item.description: The days of being just a "code monkey" are ending. If you are still memorizing boilerplate code and manually typing out standard CRUD operations, you are competing against machines that can do it...

item.title: Your guide to sci-fi streaming season
item.description: I haven't quite figured out the reason why, but for the last few years, summer has become the moment for new science fiction shows on streaming services. And 2026 isn't any different - aside from...

item.title: I BUILT A " Multi-Lingual Voice AI " called " SEGA PRO"
item.description: In the modern era of AI, we often assume that building a "Smart Assistant" requires complex backend architecture, heavy LLM integrations, and expensive API keys. But what if you could build a...

item.title: Secure File Uploads: Seven Checks and Why Each One Exists
item.description: Part 3 of 4 — Laravel Architecture Patterns for Production ~9 min read · Security · Middleware · File handling A file upload is the moment you hand control to an untrusted user. Everything else in...

item.title: Christian content creators are outsourcing AI slop to gig workers on Fiverr
item.description: In the beginning, platforms like Fiverr were places where people could hire freelancers to do specialized creative labor using skills that took years to develop. In the age of generative AI,...

item.title: Oura adds birth control support to its period tracker
item.description: Oura is launching a new reproductive health feature that takes hormonal contraception into consideration when tracking period cycles. The smart ring maker describes the Hormonal Birth Control...

item.title: The Download: a new Christian phone network, and debugging LLMs
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn...

item.title: Microsoft wants lawyers to trust its new AI agent in Word documents
item.description: Microsoft is launching a new AI agent inside Word that's specifically designed for legal teams. Legal Agent handles document edits, negotiation history, and complex documents to help legal teams...

item.title: A Ransomware Negotiator Was Working for a Ransomware Gang
item.description: Someone pleaded guilty to secretly working for a ransomware gang as he negotiated ransomware payments for clients.

item.title: Dyson finally made a better robot, but a worse vacuum
item.description: I'm deeply conflicted about the Dyson Spot + Scrub Ai robot vacuum and mop. It's the company's best robotic floor cleaner to date, with excellent mopping performance, good navigation and obstacle...

item.title: Dyson put someone else’s motor in its robot vacuum
item.description: Dyson, a company built on engineering high-speed motors, has confirmed to The Verge that its newest robot vacuum doesn't use a Dyson motor. The recently launched Spot & Scrub Ai robot vacuum and...

item.title: Top Five Sales Challenges Costing MSPs Cybersecurity Revenue
item.description: The managed security services market is projected to grow from $38.31 billion in 2025 to $69.16 billion by 2030[1], with cybersecurity being the fastest-growing sector[2]. Despite this...

item.title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
item.description: Smack dab between Australia and South America, the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) research vessel Rainier is currently on a mission to map more than 8,000 square...

item.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Get 4-Year Sentences in BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the sentencing of two cybersecurity professionals to four years each in prison for their role in facilitating BlackCat ransomware attacks...

item.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
item.description: A new software supply chain attack campaign has been observed using sleeper packages as a conduit to subsequently push malicious payloads that enabled credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering,...

item.title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
item.description: This past week delivered another gut punch for science in the US. This time, the target was the National Science Foundation—a federal agency that funds major research projects to the tune of...

item.title: A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
item.description: A new US-wide cell phone network marketed to Christians is set to launch next week. It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level...

item.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
item.description: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

item.title: How Shivon Zilis Operated as Elon Musk’s OpenAI Insider
item.description: Messages presented at trial reveal how Zilis, the mother of four of Musk’s children, acted as an intermediary between him and OpenAI.

item.title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
item.description: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Good Luck Getting a Mac Mini for the Next ‘Several Months’
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook told analysts that AI adoption has happened faster than expected.

item.title: The craziest part of Musk v. Altman happened while the jury was out of the room
item.description: Okay, I am not a lawyer so I only understood about half of what just happened. But I am fairly sure, given the context, that Elon Musk's lawyers may have just fucked up big. Jared "James...
